---
title: "GSA_CarbonRetention_Biosolid_CA"
author: "Dr. Chih-Yu Hung"
date: "2026-04-30"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup}
# --- Packages ----
library(tidyverse)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)
```

```{r data_and_function}
# --- Data preparation ----
# Goal: global sensitivity analysis of soil carbon retention after biosolid application.
# The selected uncertain parameters are biosolid N input, C/N ratio, and BCR.

set.seed(2026)
target_year <- 2015

DF <- readRDS("Inputs/BiosolidN_corrected.rds") # Data from Chang's work

Data <- DF %>%
  filter(Inv_Year_ID == target_year) %>%
  select(
    Inv_Year_ID,
    Province_ID,
    Ecodistrict_ID,
    BION_corrected
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    CN_ratio = 8.764367816, # Table 1: average C / average N.
    BCR      = 0.194,       # Biosolid carbon retention coefficient, Lin et al. 2024.
    BIOC     = BION_corrected * CN_ratio
  ) %>%
  filter(BIOC > 0) %>%
  drop_na()

# --- Soil carbon retention calculation ----
# Positive CO2e means the amount of CO2 equivalent retained in soil.

calc_carbon <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      deltaC = BIOC * BCR
    ) %>%
    summarise(
      Tot.Carbon = sum(BIOC, na.rm = TRUE),
      Tot.deltaC = sum(deltaC, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      CO2e = Tot.deltaC * 44 / 12,
      `CO2e(kt)` = CO2e / 10^6,
      CO2e_kt = `CO2e(kt)`
    )
}
```

```{r gsa_settings}
# --- GSA settings ----
# Latin hypercube sampling is used to cover the uncertainty space efficiently.
# PRCC is used because the model response is expected to be monotonic with each input.

n_gsa <- 5000
seed  <- 2026
set.seed(seed)

par_tbl <- tibble(
  par = c("BION_scale", "CN_ratio_scale", "BCR_scale"),
  mean = c(1, 1, 1),
  p95  = c(0.15, 0.26, 0.50)
)

# --- Sampling helpers ----

make_lhs <- function(n, k) {
  replicate(k, sample((seq_len(n) - runif(n)) / n)) %>%
    as_tibble(.name_repair = "minimal")
}

qnorm_ci <- function(p, mu, p95) {
  sd <- abs(p95) * abs(mu) / 1.96
  pmax(qnorm(p, mean = mu, sd = sd), 0)
}
```

```{r generate_samples}
# --- Generate Latin hypercube samples ----
# Each column is transformed to the distribution assigned to that parameter.

U <- make_lhs(n_gsa, nrow(par_tbl))
names(U) <- par_tbl$par

Sample <- U %>%
  mutate(
    BION_scale     = qnorm_ci(BION_scale, 1, par_tbl$p95[par_tbl$par == "BION_scale"]),
    CN_ratio_scale = qnorm_ci(CN_ratio_scale, 1, par_tbl$p95[par_tbl$par == "CN_ratio_scale"]),
    BCR_scale      = qnorm_ci(BCR_scale, 1, par_tbl$p95[par_tbl$par == "BCR_scale"])
  )

summary(Sample)
```

```{r run_gsa}
# --- Run GSA model ----
# Each sample applies one multiplier to all rows, representing parameter uncertainty.

run_one_gsa <- function(i, Data, Sample) {
  s <- Sample[i, ]
  
  Data %>%
    mutate(
      BION_corrected = BION_corrected * s$BION_scale,
      CN_ratio       = CN_ratio * s$CN_ratio_scale,
      BCR            = BCR * s$BCR_scale,
      BIOC           = BION_corrected * CN_ratio
    ) %>%
    calc_carbon() %>%
    mutate(
      GSA = i,
      BION_scale = s$BION_scale,
      CN_ratio_scale = s$CN_ratio_scale,
      BCR_scale = s$BCR_scale
    )
}

Result_GSA <- map_dfr(seq_len(n_gsa), run_one_gsa, Data = Data, Sample = Sample)

summary(Result_GSA) # CO2e(kt) 98.058, 100.023
saveRDS(Result_GSA, "Outputs/GSA_Carbon_Biosolid_CA.rds")
```

```{r prcc_function}
# --- PRCC function ----
# PRCC is the correlation between ranked residuals after removing the effects of other inputs.

calc_prcc <- function(df, y_col, x_cols) {
  dat <- df %>%
    select(all_of(c(y_col, x_cols))) %>%
    drop_na()
  
  map_dfr(x_cols, function(x) {
    others <- setdiff(x_cols, x)
    
    y_res <- lm(rank(dat[[y_col]]) ~ ., data = dat %>% select(all_of(others)) %>% mutate(across(everything(), rank))) %>%
      residuals()
    
    x_res <- lm(rank(dat[[x]]) ~ ., data = dat %>% select(all_of(others)) %>% mutate(across(everything(), rank))) %>%
      residuals()
    
    test <- cor.test(x_res, y_res, method = "pearson")
    
    tibble(
      variable = x,
      PRCC = unname(test$estimate),
      p.value = test$p.value
    )
  }) %>%
    mutate(
      abs_PRCC = abs(PRCC),
      contribution = PRCC^2 / sum(PRCC^2) * 100
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs_PRCC))
}
```

```{r prcc_results}
# --- Calculate PRCC ----
# CO2e_kt is used instead of `CO2e(kt)` to avoid non standard column names in helper functions.

x_cols <- c("BION_scale", "CN_ratio_scale", "BCR_scale")

PRCC_tbl <- calc_prcc(Result_GSA, "CO2e_kt", x_cols)

PRCC_tbl # Contribution, BCR, 41%   BioC = 59%
write.csv(PRCC_tbl, "Outputs/GSA_PRCC_Carbon_Biosolid_CA.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r plot_prcc}
# --- Plot PRCC ----
# The sign shows direction. The absolute value shows the strength of monotonic influence.

p_prcc <- PRCC_tbl %>%
  mutate(variable = fct_reorder(variable, PRCC)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = PRCC, y = variable)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +
  labs(
    x = "Partial rank correlation coefficient",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Global sensitivity of soil carbon retention from biosolid application"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

p_prcc

ggsave(
  "Outputs/GSA_PRCC_Carbon_Biosolid_CA.png",
  p_prcc,
  width = 7,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r plot_contribution}
# --- Plot normalized contribution ----
# Contribution is calculated from squared PRCC and sums to 100 percent.

p_contribution <- PRCC_tbl %>%
  mutate(variable = fct_reorder(variable, contribution)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = contribution, y = variable)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized squared PRCC contribution (%)",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Relative sensitivity contribution"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

p_contribution

ggsave(
  "Outputs/GSA_Contribution_Carbon_Biosolid_CA.png",
  p_contribution,
  width = 7,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)
```
